## Candidate Papers (pre-screen)

### NEW — High Priority
- C1: Andani, I.G.A., Qamilla, N., et al. (2025). "Spatial, mobility, or socio-economic inequity? A district level job accessibility evaluation in Jakarta, Indonesia." International Journal of Urban Sciences. DOI: 10.1080/12265934.2025.2553714
- C2: Gelb, J. & Alizadeh, H. (2025). "Towards fair transit: a toolbox for equity diagnostics in spatial accessibility." Public Transport. DOI: 10.1007/s12469-025-00412-y
- C3: [Authors TBC] (2026). "Enhancing urban accessibility with railway network development: a comparative scenario analysis in Bangkok, Thailand." Public Transport. DOI: 10.1007/s12469-026-00421-5

### NEW — Medium Priority
- C4: Ayuriany, T., Lee, J.H., & Hidayati, I. (2023). "What access-for-all entails? Examining commuting experiences from subjective and objective accessibility in a fast-growing city, Jakarta." Asian Transport Studies.
- C5: [Authors TBC] (2025). "Exploring the transition to electric motorcycles and its role in multimodal travel in the Jakarta metropolitan area." Discover Sustainability. DOI: 10.1007/s43621-025-01908-0

### ALREADY IN SOURCE MAP — Confirmed existing
- Rathod et al. (2025) — paper #14 already in source-map.md

### LOW RELEVANCE — Screened out
- Li et al. (2022/2025) arXiv U.S. POI accessibility — U.S. focused, 2SFCA not H3, not developing country
- Wuhan Lorenz/Gini (2023) — urban/rural China, too far from methodology
- Brazil bus equity-quality trade-off (2025) — relevant conceptually but duplicates Pereira et al. 2019 role
- Beijing multi-job transport equity (2025) — Chinese megacity, no novel methodology for our gap
